steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The researcher argues GPS records higher distances than RTD because GPS captures all actual movements, while RTD only measures twice the direct distance between two habitats. The correct choice must show GPS exceeding RTD for the same animal.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "Tibetan antelope would be less than 700 kilometers if the GPS method had been used.": Directly contradicts the researcher's claim that GPS records more. ✗
  • "caribou would be less than 4,868 kilometers if the RTD method had been used.": The table shows 4,868 km via GPS. RTD's method of doubling the direct distance would undercount, so RTD < GPS. This confirms GPS exceeded RTD for the same animal. ✓
  • "caribou would be greater than 1,325 kilometers if the RTD method had been used.": This does not establish that caribou's RTD distance would be lower than its 4,868 km GPS distance; it compares caribou RTD to the brown bear's GPS value instead. ✗
  • "Tibetan antelope would be greater than 4,868 kilometers if the GPS method had been used.": The 4,868 km figure is the caribou GPS distance; the table gives no basis for claiming Tibetan antelope GPS distance would exceed that. ✗
That matches the correct answer — B is correct.
